title: Root Causes
summary: Root causes behind discovery problems include weak exploration tools, over-personalization, playlist dependency, ad disruption on the free tier, and feedback signals that fail to tune recommendations.

evidence.label: Ad disruption (free tier)
evidence.support_line: 87 reviews across App Store and Play Store

evidence.label: Weak exploration tools
evidence.support_line: 63 reviews across Reddit and Social Media

evidence.label: Performance & stability
evidence.support_line: 58 reviews across Play Store, App Store, Reddit and Social Media

evidence.label: Playlist dependency
evidence.support_line: 53 reviews across Social Media, Community Forums, Reddit and Play Store

evidence.label: Over-personalization
evidence.support_line: 14 reviews across App Store and Social Media

root_causes: Ad disruption (free tier): Free-tier ads break the listening flow and undermine intentional exploration
Weak exploration tools: There are not enough surfaces, controls, or settings to actively go discover something new
Performance & stability: Crashes, lag, sync issues, or login failures interrupt listening before discovery can happen
Playlist dependency: Listeners can only discover through Discover Weekly / Release Radar / Daily Mix and have no alternatives
Over-personalization: Algorithm leans so hard on a user's history that it stops surfacing anything unfamiliar
affected_segments: Discovery Seeker: Hard to find new artists or genres
Playlist User: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Heavy Listener: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Casual Listener: Ads disrupt the discovery flow
Premium User: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Free User: Ads disrupt the discovery flow
unmet_needs: Need dedicated discovery surfaces beyond Discover Weekly
Need better personalization that learns from feedback
Need fresher and more accurate recommendations
recommended_actions: Fix systemic causes (over-personalization, weak exploration tools) before surface tweaks.
Assign owners to the top root-cause categories with OKRs tied to review sentiment.
Run root-cause reviews when discovery NPS or complaint volume shifts.
